spec_match.rationale: Like multiple-mirrors, this demo places the mirror <selectedcontent> in container B outside of <select id='picker'>. Even when the select is appended to container B, the mirror is a sibling rather than a descendant, which breaks native syncing behavior.

interactive.rationale: Allows moving the select between container A and B, and adding/removing options, while logging MutationObserver records.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Correctly explains that mutations should be deferred until the surrounding DOM step finishes.

edge_cases.rationale: Attaches MutationObserver instances to all <selectedcontent> elements, but observer #1 will never trigger because it is outside the select.

references.rationale: Correctly cross-references the feature index and companion multiple-mirrors demo.

design_system.rationale: Good use of interactive state buttons, clear layouts, and monospace log boxes.

openQuestions.title: Correct mirror position in container B
openQuestions.detail: The mirror in container B must be a descendant of <select> (e.g. inside a <button> wrapper or slot subtree) to receive native updates and observe the correct mutation timing.

summary: An observer timing demonstration that is structurally flawed due to placing one of its observed <selectedcontent> elements outside the target select, rendering it inactive.
